# PERSONA — Design Spec

> A self-updating dynamic NFT that reads your wallet's on-chain history and mints
> an AI-written personality dossier — which then evolves on its own over time.
>
> Built for the Encode Club **Somnia Agentathon** (May 20 – June 10, 2026).

---

## 1. Overview

**PERSONA** turns a crypto wallet's on-chain history into a short, witty
"personality dossier" generated by an on-chain LLM, minted as a soulbound NFT.
The dossier is not static: a scheduled on-chain agent re-reads the wallet and
rewrites the dossier over time, so the NFT *evolves* as the owner's behavior
changes — with no off-chain server in the loop.

It is deliberately built as a **viral, shareable artifact**: each dossier is
unique to its wallet, a little roasty, and screenshot-friendly.

### Why this fits Somnia specifically

Somnia's own developer guide lists "Self-Updating Dynamic NFTs" as a canonical
agentic use case: *"JSON API Request fetches the data; LLM Inference interprets
it into a trait or rarity update. Every change is on-chain and verifiable."*
PERSONA is exactly this pattern, made personal and social.

The project is **Somnia-native** because two of its mechanics cannot run on a
plain EVM chain without a trusted off-chain server:

1. **Consensus-validated AI generation** — the dossier text is produced by
   Somnia's validator-executed LLM (Qwen3-30B, temp=0, fixed seed). No central
   party can fake or alter a reading after the fact.
2. **Autonomous evolution** — a Cron-Reactivity subscription re-generates
   dossiers on a schedule with no keeper bot, no cron server, no private API key.

---

## 2. Goals & non-goals

### Goals
- Ship a complete, deployed, demoable end-to-end project on Somnia Shannon testnet.
- Use the canonical agent pattern (JSON API + LLM Inference) plus Reactivity.
- Produce a shareable artifact that a non-crypto person immediately "gets".
- Be finishable solo, by a beginner using Claude Code, in ~3 weeks.

### Non-goals (explicitly out of scope)
- Multi-chain wallet reading (Somnia testnet only for this version).
- Generated SVG/image art in the NFT (text dossier only; styled on the frontend).
- Social features (likes, comments, search) in the gallery.
- Automated on-chain transactions on the user's behalf.
- Mainnet deployment.

---

## 3. User experience

### Primary flow
1. User opens the web app and pastes (or connects) a wallet address.
2. App calls the PERSONA contract; the contract dispatches two agent requests.
3. A loader shows the agents working (~30s): "Reading the chain… asking the
   validators…".
4. The dossier card renders:

   ```
   ─────────────────────────────────────────────
     Your dossier · May 20, 2026          ★★★☆☆
   ─────────────────────────────────────────────
     TYPE: Testnet Drifter, Type II
     STRENGTH: Relentless — 87 txs in 23 days, you don't sleep
     WEAKNESS: Spreads thin across 14 contracts, master of none
     STYLE: "I'll just try this one more dApp"
     KARMA: +7
     NOTES: Classic early-explorer energy. Holds a couple NFTs but
            no conviction bags. The wallet of someone hunting alpha.
     RARITY: 3
   ─────────────────────────────────────────────
                  [ Mint NFT · 1.0 STT ]
   ```

5. User clicks **Mint**. A soulbound ERC-721 is minted to their address with the
   dossier stored in the token's dynamic `tokenURI`.
6. **First 100 minters are fully refunded** (effectively free), funded from the
   contract's STT reserve.

### Evolution flow (the hook)
- On a schedule (monthly in production), a Cron-Reactivity subscription fires.
- The contract re-reads each registered wallet and regenerates its dossier,
  updating the stored text and incrementing a `generation` counter.
- The owner opens their NFT later and sees a *different* dossier reflecting their
  new on-chain behavior — with no action on their part.

### Gallery flow (stretch)
- Every read/evolution is published to a Somnia Data Stream `personas-feed`.
- A gallery page subscribes to the stream and shows recent dossiers.

---

## 4. Architecture

```
[ User + wallet ]
        |
        v
[ Next.js frontend on Vercel ] --- viem --->
        |
        v
+------------------------------------------------------+
|        PERSONA contract (Somnia Shannon testnet)     |
|                                                      |
|  read(address):                                      |
|    1. JSON API Request agent  -> wallet stats        |
|    2. LLM Inference agent      -> dossier text        |
|    -> store dossier, mint soulbound NFT              |
|                                                      |
|  Cron-Reactivity subscription:                       |
|    monthly -> re-read each wallet -> update dossier  |
|                                                      |
|  (stretch) publish each dossier to Data Stream       |
+------------------------------------------------------+
        |                              ^
        v (agent calls)                | (async callbacks)
[ Somnia Agents platform 0x037Bb9C718F3f7fe5eCBDB0b600D607b52706776 ]
        |
        v
[ Validator subcommittee: fetch + run Qwen3-30B + consensus ]
```

All user-facing logic and state live on Somnia. External data (the explorer
API) is pulled *into* the chain by the JSON API agent; no off-chain backend.

---

## 5. Smart contract design

### 5.1 Standard & libraries
- Solidity `0.8.24`, compiled with `viaIR: true`, optimizer runs 200.
- OpenZeppelin `ERC721`.
- ERC-5192 (minimal soulbound) — `locked(tokenId)` returns true; transfers revert.

### 5.2 State

```solidity
mapping(address => uint256) public personaOf;     // wallet -> tokenId (0 = none)
mapping(uint256 => string)  public dossier;       // tokenId -> dossier text
mapping(uint256 => uint256) public lastUpdated;   // tokenId -> timestamp
mapping(uint256 => uint256) public generation;    // tokenId -> version (1 = first read)
address[] public registeredWallets;               // for Cron iteration
uint256 public totalPersonas;
uint256 public freeMintsRemaining;                // starts at 100

// agent request bookkeeping
mapping(uint256 => RequestCtx) internal requests; // agent requestId -> context
```

`RequestCtx` tracks which wallet/tokenId an in-flight agent request belongs to
and which stage of the pipeline it is (stats fetch vs. dossier generation).

### 5.3 Core functions

- `read(address wallet) payable` — entry point. Requires `personaOf[wallet] == 0`
  (one-per-wallet). Requires `msg.value >= MINT_PRICE` (1.0 STT). Kicks off the
  agent pipeline (see §6). NFT is minted in the final callback once the dossier
  is ready.
- `handleStats(...)` — callback for the JSON API agent. Decodes stats, then
  dispatches the LLM Inference request.
- `handleDossier(...)` — callback for the LLM agent. Stores the dossier text,
  mints the soulbound NFT (first read) or updates it (evolution), refunds the
  first 100 minters, publishes to the Data Stream (stretch).
- `tokenURI(uint256)` — builds a data-URI JSON on the fly from `dossier[id]`,
  `generation[id]`, `lastUpdated[id]`. Always reflects the latest dossier.
- `reread(uint256 tokenId)` — re-runs the agent pipeline for a single existing
  NFT and updates its dossier (increments `generation`). This is the MVP path
  that proves the dynamic-NFT concept without Cron, and is also the per-token
  unit of work that `evolveAll()` calls internally. Open to the owner (and, when
  funded from reserve, to the Cron trigger).
- `evolveAll()` — Cron-triggered. Iterates registered wallets in batches of
  5–10 per call (to stay under block gas limit), calling the same per-token
  re-read logic, self-chaining via a one-shot Cron subscription until all are
  processed (see §7).
- `refuel(uint256 tokenId) payable` — anyone can top up the reserve to pay for a
  specific NFT's next evolution if the global reserve is exhausted.

### 5.4 Economics

| Action | Payer | Amount | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| First read + mint | user | 1.0 STT | Covers ~0.36–0.48 STT agent cost + reserve for future evolutions |
| First 100 reads | contract | refund 1.0 STT | Funded from an initial reserve seeded at deploy |
| Monthly evolution | contract | ~0.36 STT / NFT | From accumulated reserve; if exhausted, NFT skipped until `refuel` |
| Base gas | user | ~negligible | Standard tx fee |

Agent deposit formula (critical): for every agent call,
`msg.value >= getRequestDeposit() + pricePerAgent * subcommitteeSize`.
Per-validator prices: JSON API 0.03, LLM Inference 0.07 STT; default
subcommittee = 3. Unused deposit is auto-refunded — the contract MUST implement
`receive() external payable`.

### 5.5 Integration safety rules (baked in from day 1)
- `receive() external payable` present (or agent rebates silently fail).
- Every agent callback gated: `require(msg.sender == address(platform))` and
  `require(requests[requestId].active)`.
- Handle all `ResponseStatus` values (Success / Failed / TimedOut). Only decode
  `responses[0].result` on `Success`; otherwise mark the request failed and let
  the user retry without re-paying.

---

## 6. AI pipeline & prompts

The flow follows Somnia's canonical "data + reasoning" pattern.

### Step 1 — fetch wallet stats (JSON API Request agent)
Target: Shannon explorer REST API (Blockscout, `/api/v2/addresses/{address}` and
related endpoints). Pull: native balance, transaction count, account age, unique
contracts interacted with, tokens held, NFTs held.

> **Day-1 spike / Risk #1:** confirm the explorer exposes a public REST API
> usable by the JSON API agent, and how many `fetch*` calls are needed (each call
> reads one selector). If a single compound field isn't available, either make a
> small number of calls or fall back to the **Parse Website** agent (one call,
> ~0.33 STT, reads the address page directly).

### Step 2 — generate the dossier (LLM Inference agent, `inferChat`)

**System prompt:**
```
You are PERSONA, a witty on-chain analyst. You read a crypto wallet's
activity and write a short, punchy personality dossier. Be clever and a
little roasty, but never hateful: no slurs, never target a person's real-world
identity, religion, or ethnicity. Roast their on-chain behavior only.
You MUST output in the exact template given. One line per field. No preamble.
```

**User prompt (stats injected by the contract):**
```
Wallet: 0x...
On-chain stats (Somnia Shannon testnet):
- Native balance: <X> STT
- Total transactions: <N>
- Account age: <D> days
- Unique contracts used: <C>
- Tokens held: <T>
- NFTs held: <F>

Write the dossier in EXACTLY this format:

TYPE: <creative archetype name>, Type <I-V>
STRENGTH: <one line>
WEAKNESS: <one line>
STYLE: "<a short quote capturing their vibe>"
KARMA: <signed number, your judgment>
NOTES: <1-2 sentences of witty observation about their on-chain behavior>
RARITY: <1-5>
```

The contract stores the full returned string. The frontend parses it line-by-line
(`TYPE:`, `STRENGTH:`, …) to render the styled card, with a graceful fallback that
shows the raw text if a line is missing.

### Determinism
Qwen3-30B at temp=0 with a fixed seed means identical stats produce an identical
dossier. This is desirable: readings are reproducible, evolution is meaningful
(text changes only when on-chain history changes), and validator consensus holds.

---

## 7. Evolution mechanic (Cron-Reactivity)

- A Cron subscription fires on a monthly cadence (production). For the demo, a
  short interval (every N blocks) is used so evolution can be shown live.
- `evolveAll()` processes `registeredWallets` in batches of 5–10 to respect block
  gas limits, then re-subscribes a one-shot Cron for the next block until the
  batch cursor reaches `totalPersonas`.
- Each wallet in a batch triggers the same two-step agent pipeline as a first
  read; the final callback updates the stored dossier and increments `generation`.
- If the reserve can't fund a wallet's evolution, that wallet is skipped and an
  `EvolutionSkipped` event is emitted (frontend prompts the owner to `refuel`).

> **Risk #2:** Cron-Reactivity is the most complex piece. The MVP does not depend
> on it — a manual `read`-again path proves the dynamic-NFT concept. Cron is
> layered on in week 2; if it stalls, the demo triggers evolution manually or via
> a short interval.

---

## 8. Frontend

- Next.js (App Router), viem, deployed on Vercel.
- Pages:
  - **Home / Read** — address input (or wallet connect), loader, dossier card, mint button.
  - **My Persona** — view your minted NFT, its current dossier and generation.
  - **Gallery (stretch)** — recent dossiers from the Data Stream.
- The loader explicitly narrates the agent steps to make the on-chain AI visible
  (this is the core "wow" for the demo video).

---

## 9. Scope

### MVP (must-have for submission)
1. `read` → JSON API + LLM Inference → store dossier.
2. Soulbound NFT mint with dynamic `tokenURI`.
3. Manual re-read that updates an existing dossier (proves the dynamic concept).
4. Frontend: read → loader → card → mint.
5. Deployed + verified on Somnia testnet.
6. First-100 refund.

### Somnia hook (high priority — week 2)
7. Cron-Reactivity automated evolution.

### Stretch (if time remains)
8. Data Streams gallery.
9. Rarity-based rewards (rare types get a bonus/tag; needs one extra agent call).
10. On-chain SVG art instead of plain text.

---

## 10. Timeline (kickoff May 20; target submission ~June 7, buffer to June 10)

**Week 1 (May 20–26) — foundation**
- Day 1: scaffold (Hardhat + Next.js + Somnia config); verify explorer API;
  first successful agent call (BTC-oracle pattern from Somnia's guide).
- Day 2–3: contract read flow (JSON API + LLM) → store dossier.
- Day 4–5: soulbound NFT mint + dynamic `tokenURI`.
- Day 6–7: minimal frontend; full flow end-to-end.

**Week 2 (May 27 – June 2) — hook + robustness**
- Day 8–10: Cron-Reactivity evolution.
- Day 11–12: first-100 refund, edge cases (empty wallet, agent failure, dedup).
- Day 13–14: frontend polish — card design, loader animation.

**Week 3 (June 3–10) — ship**
- Day 15–16: Data Streams gallery (stretch) or extra polish.
- Day 17: final deploy + verify contracts.
- Day 18–19: record demo video (multiple takes).
- Day 20: README + landing copy (English).
- Day 21 (~June 7): final commit + submit; buffer to June 10.

---

## 11. Risks & mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Explorer API not usable by JSON API agent | medium | Day-1 spike; fall back to Parse Website |
| Cron-Reactivity proves hard | medium | MVP doesn't depend on it; manual re-read fallback; short interval for demo |
| Qwen3-30B breaks output format | low | Tight prompt + frontend parsing fallback |
| Agent latency/cost surprises | low | Buffer built into 1.0 STT mint |
| First hackathon — everything slower | high | 3-day end buffer; MVP separated from stretch |

---

## 12. Tech stack

- **Contracts:** Solidity 0.8.24, Hardhat, `viaIR: true`, OpenZeppelin ERC-721 + ERC-5192.
- **Agents:** Somnia Agents platform `0x037Bb9C718F3f7fe5eCBDB0b600D607b52706776`
  (testnet). JSON API agent id `13174292974160097713`; LLM Inference agent id
  `12847293847561029384`.
- **Frontend:** Next.js (App Router), viem, Vercel.
- **Network:** Somnia Shannon testnet — chain ID 50312, RPC
  `https://api.infra.testnet.somnia.network`, explorer
  `https://shannon-explorer.somnia.network`.

---

## 13. Open questions / day-1 spikes
- Confirm Shannon explorer REST API shape and how many JSON API calls a full
  stats pull needs (drives exact per-read cost).
- Confirm `inferChat` vs `inferString` is the better fit for the dossier (chat
  gives more room; string with no constraints may suffice and be cheaper to wire).
- Confirm Cron subscription API surface and the minimum owner balance / gas
  parameters required for on-chain reactivity.
